title: My First Interview
opening: Hello, I'm [Interviewer's Name]. Welcome to the interview. Please, tell me a little about yourself.

t1.prompt: Hello, I'm [Interviewer's Name]. Welcome to the interview. Please, tell me a little about yourself.
t1.promptTranslation: Hello (Hel-lo), I'm (I-m) [Interviewer's Name]. Welcome (Wel-kum) to the (too thuh) interview (in-ter-vyoo). Please (pleez), tell (tel) me a (mee uh) little (li-tl) about (uh-bowt) yourself (yor-self).

t2.prompt: Thank you. What kind of experience do you have? Or what skills do you bring to this job?
t2.promptTranslation: Thank you (Thank yoo). What (wot) kind (kynd) of experience (eks-pee-ree-ens) do you (doo yoo) have (hav)? Or what (or wot) skills (skilz) do you (doo yoo) bring (bring) to this (too this) job (job)?

t3.prompt: That's great. Can you tell me about one of your strengths, and one area you want to improve?
t3.promptTranslation: That's great (That's grate). Can you (Kan yoo) tell me (tel mee) about (uh-bowt) one (wun) of your (ov yor) strengths (strengths), and one (and wun) area (air-ee-uh) you want (yoo wont) to improve (too im-proov)?

t4.prompt: Thank you. Do you have any questions for me about the company or the job?
t4.promptTranslation: Thank you (Thank yoo). Do you (doo yoo) have (hav) any (en-ee) questions (kwes-chunz) for me (for mee) about (uh-bowt) the company (thuh kum-pah-nee) or the (or thuh) job (job)?

t-repeat.prompt: Sorry, could you please repeat that? I didn't quite catch it.
t-repeat.promptTranslation: Sorry (Sor-ree), could you (kood yoo) please (pleez) repeat (ree-peet) that (that)? I didn't (did-nt) quite (kwyt) catch (katch) it.

t-end.prompt: Thank you for your time today. We'll be in touch soon. Goodbye!
t-end.promptTranslation: Thank you (Thank yoo) for your (for yor) time (tym) today (too-day). We'll be (weel bee) in touch (in tutch) soon (soon). Goodbye (Good-by)!
